title: Cephalocereus guerreronis
intro: Cephalocereus guerreronis, also known as the 'Golden Ball Cactus', is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cephalocereus guerreronis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cephalocereus guerreronis is commonly used to make infusions, teas, and ointments, and is also used in the preparation of tinctures and extracts.
taxonomy: Cephalocereus guerreronis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically in the phylum Magnoliophyta. It falls under the class Magnoliopsida and is further classified as an order Caryophyllales. In terms of family, Cephalocereus guerreronis is part of the family Cactaceae. Its genus, Cephalocereus, is a group of cacti native to the Americas. Cephalocereus guerreronis is a species of cactus within this genus.

parts: Cephalocereus guerreronis is a species of cactus native to Mexico and the Caribbean. Cephalocereus guerreronis is used in traditional medicine, and its medicinal parts are reportedly its fruits and stems.
